title: centaurium maritimum var. brevipes
intro: Centaurium maritimum var. brevipes, also known as Short-toed Centaury, is a rare plant species native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa, characterized by its distinctive blue flowers and short stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its rich content of iridoid glycosides, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Centaurium maritimum var. brevipes include its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its ability to soothe respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
